source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An electric circuit consists of two resistors (resistances $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$), a single condenser (capacitor $C$) and a variable voltage source $V$ joined together as shown in Fig. 3.83. When $V(t)$ is a very sharp pulse at $t=0$, we approximate $V(t)=A\delta(t)$. What is the time history of the potential drop across $R_{1}$ ?
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干显式写明“as shown in Fig. 3.83”，电路连接关系依赖配图
requires_image=true 且 text_dominant=false，说明关键信息不主要在文字中
要求求解 R1 两端电压的时间历程，必须先从图中读取电路拓扑
multi_step: 需要先从图中识别 R1、R2、C 与电压源的连接方式，再建立瞬态方程
题目给出 V(t)=Aδ(t) 的脉冲近似，需结合电路拓扑与冲激输入分析指数衰减响应
答案不仅是定性判断，还包含具体衰减率 \((R1+R2)/(CR1R2)\)，通常涉及多步推导
verifiability: answer_type=short_text，答案形式较明确，可核查是否为指数衰减并带有 t>0/t<0 分段
answer_text 给出了可比对的解析结构：对 t>0 为 exp(-kt)，对 t<0 为 0
registry_passed=true 且 answer_present=true，样本资产完整，便于后续规则或符号级核验

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: An electric circuit consists of two resistors (resistances $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$), a single condenser (capacitor $C$) and a variable voltage source $V$ joined together as shown in Fig. 3.83. When $V(t)$ is a very sharp pulse at $t=0$, we approximate $V(t)=A\delta(t)$. What is the time history of the potential drop across $R_{1}$ ?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: When $V(t)=A\delta(t)$, use the relation $\delta(t)=\frac{1}{2\pi}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{i\omega t}d\omega$. The voltage drop across $R_{1}$ is written as $V_{1}=\frac{A}{2\pi}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\frac{R_{1}(R_{2}-\frac{i}{\omega C})}{R_{1}R_{2}-\frac{i}{\omega C}(R_{1}+R_{2})}e^{i\omega t}d\omega=\frac{A}{2\pi}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\frac{\left(\omega-\frac{i}{R_{2}C}\right)}{(\omega-\omega_{1})}e^{i\omega t}d\omega$, where $\omega_{1}=i\frac{R_{1}+R_{2}}{CR_{1}R_{2}}$. Using the residue theorem, the solution is found to be $V_{1}\propto \exp\left(i\omega_{1}t\right)=\exp\left(-\frac{R_{1}+R_{2}}{CR_{1}R_{2}}t\right)$ for $t>0$, with $V_{1}=0$ for $t<0$.
